paragraphs.text: Two major pieces should put a decent part into the DFA: For one, purchased items should be owned by me and I should be able to do with it as I please. Paying for a product versus paying for a service gives me right and ownership. Thus, if I am buying (for example) a video game, I should be able to make use of this game as I see fit. While I shouldn't need to know how a game or a piece of software is created (e.g. source code, dependencies), I certainly should be able to use it whenever and wherever I want after purchase. My copy is my own. Another significant piece is how modern payment processor want to dictate through second-hand pressure (being a service provider to another company) what should and shouldn't be put onto the market. While the initial push was reasonable (violence towards women), it might open up more unreasonable avenues in the future. Payment processors should not be deciding what is the law or what is following the law.
